title: Senior Sales Manager
summary: Own a portfolio of two to three projects end to end — from first enquiry through booking and handover — leading a small team of advisors.
responsibilities: Own the sales number for an assigned set of projects, and the forecast behind it.
Lead, coach and review a team of four to six sales advisors.
Run site visits personally for high-intent buyers and close negotiations within the published price sheet.
Work with CRM to keep post-booking documentation moving without buyer chasing.
Feed market intelligence back into pricing and launch planning.
requirements: Six or more years in residential or commercial real estate sales, with at least two leading a team.
A track record you can evidence — bookings closed, not just enquiries handled.
Comfort working to a fixed, published price sheet rather than discretionary discounting.

title: Site Civil Engineer
summary: Run day-to-day execution on an active site, with direct accountability for quality checks at every slab.
responsibilities: Supervise daily site execution against the approved drawings and programme.
Run quality checks at each stage and maintain the third-party audit trail.
Coordinate contractors, material flow and safety compliance on site.
Track progress against the published construction timeline and escalate slippage early.
Maintain accurate site records for the RERA quarterly filing.
requirements: B.E. or B.Tech in Civil Engineering.
Three or more years on active residential or commercial sites.
Working knowledge of AutoCAD and standard project scheduling tools.
A bias for flagging problems early rather than absorbing them quietly.

title: Digital Marketing Lead
summary: Own demand generation across search, social and performance channels — measured on qualified leads and cost per booking, not impressions.
responsibilities: Plan and run paid campaigns across Google and Meta for project launches.
Own SEO and content strategy for the website and blog.
Instrument and report on GA4 events, attribution and cost per qualified lead.
Work with the sales desk to close the loop between lead source and booking.
Manage agency partners and the creative pipeline.
requirements: Four or more years in performance marketing, ideally in real estate or another considered-purchase category.
Hands-on with GA4, Google Ads and Meta Ads Manager.
Comfortable being measured on cost per booking rather than traffic.

title: CRM Executive
summary: Be the named point of contact for buyers after booking — documentation, payment milestones and construction updates.
responsibilities: Act as the relationship manager for an assigned set of buyers from booking to handover.
Manage allotment letters, agreements and payment-milestone communication.
Share construction progress updates proactively, before buyers have to ask.
Resolve queries within committed response times and escalate what you cannot.
Keep CRM records accurate and current.
requirements: Two or more years in customer success, CRM or post-sales in real estate.
Excellent written and spoken communication in English and Hindi.
Genuine patience — this role exists to remove anxiety from a large purchase.
Working knowledge of a CRM platform.
